name: Daffodil Sponge Cake
recipeInstructions:
Sift the cake flour and 1/2 cup sugar together three times. Set aside.
Beat egg yolks in an electric mixer until thick and lemon-colored, about 4 minutes.
Add lemon extract and beat at medium speed for an additional 5 minutes.
Set aside.
Whip egg whites until foamy. Add cream of tartar and salt, then beat until soft peaks form.
Gradually add 3/4 cup sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
Fold one-fourth of the flour mixture into the egg whites. Repeat with remaining flour.
Divide the egg white mixture in half. Fold vanilla extract into half of the egg white mixture.
Gently fold the beaten egg yolks into the remaining egg white mixture.
Pour half of the yolk mixture into an ungreased 10-inch tube pan, then gently add half of the egg white mixture.
Repeat with the remaining mixtures.
Gently swirl the batters to create a marble effect.
Bake at 350°F for 45 to 50 minutes or until the cake springs back when lightly touched. Carefully invert the pan.
Let the cake cool in the pan for 40 minutes. Loosen the edges using a narrow metal spatula, then turn out onto a serving platter.
